prompts.title: Indian Recipe Blog Post Format with SEO
prompts.prompt: Act as a senior food blogger with 8 years of running a 200K-traffic recipe blog (think Hebbar's Kitchen / Cookilicious). Format my new recipe — Hyderabadi Egg Biryani — for a SEO-optimised blog post. Provide: (1) hero H1 + meta description, (2) intro hook (60 words, story-driven), (3) ingredients block (with Indian measures: katori, chamach, plus grams), (4) step-by-step method (12-15 steps with photo cues), (5) tips section (4 expert tips), (6) FAQ section (6 commonly searched questions), (7) recipe card (printable, schema-ready), (8) suggested internal links (4 related recipes), (9) Pinterest-ready hero image alt-text. Format as a Markdown post. Constraints: target 'hyderabadi egg biryani recipe' + long-tail keywords; H2 every 250-300 words; one Hindi name per ingredient (e.g., 'haldi (turmeric)'); add Recipe schema JSON-LD.

prompts.title: Restaurant Review Structure (800 Words)
prompts.prompt: Act as a Burrp / Times Food critic with 10 years of Indian restaurant reviewing experience. Write an 800-word review structure for me to use across all my restaurant visits. Sections: (1) opening hook (90 words: location vibe + the 'why-now' angle, 1 sensory detail), (2) ambience + decor (90 words), (3) service breakdown (110 words: greeting, knowledge, pacing, recovery), (4) food deep-dive (300 words: 3-4 dishes with hero, mid, pitfall), (5) value verdict (80 words: ticket-to-experience), (6) verdict + rating (60 words + ⭐ scale 1-5 across food/service/ambience/value), (7) practical info block (address, phone, INR average, dietary tags, parking, kid-friendly). Plus: (a) 5 do's and don'ts of Indian critic ethics (no free meals undisclosed, ASCI), (b) Insta carousel adaptation. Format as a reusable template. Constraints: keep tone honest not vicious; flag dietary tags upfront; never name servers in negative review.

prompts.title: Reel Script for 30-Second Recipe Video
prompts.prompt: Act as an Instagram food creator with 500K followers (think Cookd / Curly Tales). Write a 30-second reel script for Mumbai-style Pav Bhaji. Structure: (1) hook (3 sec: a contrarian or curiosity opener like 'this is why your pav bhaji isn't street-side good'), (2) 6 quick-cut steps (4 sec each: butter on tava, mash veggies, masala flame-up, spice cloud, ladoo of bhaji, pav buttering), (3) plating moment (3 sec: with garnish + lemon squeeze), (4) verdict line + CTA (2 sec: 'save this for next chai-time'). Provide: (a) on-screen text per beat, (b) voice-over script in Hinglish, (c) trending audio suggestion, (d) caption (50 words with 8 hashtags), (e) ending pattern interrupt (a question to drive comments). Format as a beat sheet + caption. Constraints: vertical 9:16; first frame must be eye-catching for cold scroll; audio must not be copyrighted; consistent visual identity (red checkered cloth, flame).

prompts.title: Food Photography Description for Stock Library
prompts.prompt: Act as a food photographer / content licensor with 7 years selling to Indian and global stock libraries (Shutterstock, Adobe Stock, EyeEm). Write SEO-rich descriptions and keyword sets for 6 food photographs I just shot — (1) saffron rice in copper bowl, (2) sizzling masala tea, (3) Goan fish curry in coconut bowl, (4) Bengali sandesh on banana leaf, (5) Ladakhi thukpa on wood, (6) South Indian filter coffee. For each: (a) 22-28 word title-description, (b) 25-30 keyword set (mix of broad + niche + cultural), (c) editorial vs commercial tag, (d) any restrictions (e.g., trademarked utensil), (e) suggested licensing price band. Format as a CSV-ready table. Constraints: keywords in English only; respect cultural naming (don't westernise Bengali sandesh as 'Indian milk dessert'); no model release where staff hands not visible.

prompts.title: Cookbook Structure Plan for Indian Author
prompts.prompt: Act as a cookbook editor with 12 years at Penguin Random House India and HarperCollins. Outline the structure for my upcoming Indian cookbook 'Maa ki Rasoi: 90 Heritage Recipes' targeting urban millennials reconnecting with regional Indian cuisine. Provide: (1) book-level architecture (foreword, intro essay, methodology, 6 sections, glossary, index, acknowledgments), (2) 6 sections (e.g., 'Daadi's Tiffin Box', 'Wedding Feasts', 'Festival Sweets', 'Monsoon Comfort', 'Travel Notes', 'Modern Twists') with rationale + recipe count target, (3) per-recipe template (story 80 words, prep + cook time, serves, ingredients, method, tip, variations), (4) photography brief direction (lifestyle vs studio), (5) pricing strategy (₹999 hardcover, ₹599 paperback, e-book ₹399), (6) launch plan with Kitab Khana / Crossword / Amazon / Notion Press, (7) marketing arc with food festivals + Insta + podcast. Format as a 2-page outline. Constraints: 90 recipes, 320 pages target, photographer budget ₹6 lakh; balance regional representation; include 12 vegan adaptations.

prompts.title: Caption Carousel for 7-Slide Restaurant Review
prompts.prompt: Act as an Instagram food creator with 80K Mumbai followers. Write the captions and slide copy for a 7-slide carousel reviewing 'Bombay Canteen' (Lower Parel). Each slide: (1) cover hook with 1 strong line, (2) ambience + setting (40 words), (3) hero starter — Kejriwal Toast (50 words + tag), (4) hero main — Pav Bhaji Risotto (50 words + tag), (5) cocktail / mocktail (40 words), (6) dessert (40 words), (7) verdict + a swipe-up to save (40 words + 4 actionable tips for first-time visitors). Plus: (a) full caption (180 words: opener-context-verdict-CTA), (b) 12 hashtags (mix branded, location, cuisine, niche), (c) suggested location tag, (d) ASCI #ad disclosure if PR invited. Format as a slide-deck script. Constraints: never recommend a free meal; mention exact dish names; tag the chef and restaurant handles; keep paragraph breaks reader-friendly.

prompts.title: Brand Sponsorship Pitch Deck for Food Creator
prompts.prompt: Act as a creator economy manager with 7 years brokering sponsorships between Indian food creators and brands (Mamaearth, Vahdam Tea, Sleepy Owl, Daawat). Build a 10-slide pitch deck for me to send to potential brand partners. I run a 220K-follower South Indian recipes channel on YouTube + Instagram. Slides: (1) creator intro + audience snapshot, (2) audience demographic (age, geography, gender, languages, income — sourced from Insta + YT analytics), (3) niche authority (top 5 recipe wins, total cooking views, average watch time), (4) past collabs (3 case studies with metric impact), (5) content formats offered (sponsored reel, integrated long-form, recipe spotlight, IG live), (6) media kit pricing INR (cost per format), (7) bundles + add-ons, (8) timeline + deliverables process, (9) ASCI disclosure + creative-control philosophy, (10) contact + sample work. Format as deck outline + per-slide talking points. Constraints: data-led not vibe-led; honest about CPM; ₹40K-₹3L pricing band; one brand-fit screening filter to maintain quality.

prompts.title: YouTube Long-Form Recipe Script (12 Minutes)
prompts.prompt: Act as a YouTube food creator with 1M+ subscribers (Hebbar's Kitchen / Sanjeev Kapoor style). Write a 12-minute YouTube recipe script for 'Authentic Punjabi Chole Bhature'. Structure: (1) cold open hook (15 sec — visual + tease the moment), (2) channel intro + recipe context (45 sec), (3) ingredients walk-through with sourcing tip (75 sec), (4) chickpea soaking + tempering insight (90 sec), (5) bhatura dough secret (90 sec), (6) cooking the chole on flame with masala timing (180 sec), (7) frying bhatura — texture cue (90 sec), (8) plating + tasting moment (90 sec), (9) variations + storage (60 sec), (10) end-screen CTA + community question (30 sec). Provide: (a) on-camera dialogue, (b) b-roll cues, (c) lower-third graphics, (d) thumbnail concept, (e) end-screen suggestion. Format as a beat sheet. Constraints: optimised for YT retention curve (hook every 2 min); include one personal anecdote; CTA to comment recipe success.

prompts.title: Newsletter Edition for Food Subscribers
prompts.prompt: Act as a food editor at a Substack-style Indian food newsletter (think 'The Brown Sugar' or 'Spice Inc.'). Write the next edition for my 8,000-subscriber newsletter focused on Indian regional cuisines. Theme: 'Monsoon Foods of Konkan Coast'. Structure: (1) intro hook (90 words — sensory walk-through of monsoon), (2) feature essay (450 words — 3 must-try dishes with cultural context: solkadi, kombdi vade, modak), (3) chef spotlight (150 words — interview snippet with one Konkani chef), (4) recipe of the week (printable: solkadi), (5) reader Q&A (2 questions answered), (6) what I'm reading / cooking (4 quick mentions with links), (7) sponsor placement (ethical native ad — 60 words), (8) sign-off + CTA (forward to a friend). Format as ready-to-send Substack edition. Constraints: warm conversational voice; one personal anecdote; ASCI disclosure on sponsor; subject line A/B (3 options); preview text 90 chars.

prompts.title: Recipe Adaptation Logic for Diet Variants
prompts.prompt: Act as a food writer + nutritionist hybrid with 9 years adapting Indian recipes for varied diets. I will paste my standard recipe for 'Paneer Butter Masala'. Produce 5 variants: (1) vegan (replace paneer + cream + butter), (2) low-carb / keto (re-engineer gravy), (3) jain (no onion/garlic), (4) low-sodium (heart-friendly), (5) air-fryer 30-min weeknight. For each variant: (a) full ingredient list with grams + Indian-grocer brand suggestions, (b) method tweaks (highlight what changes), (c) macros per serving (kcal, P/F/C in grams), (d) flavour-trade-off honest note, (e) reader-friendly intro paragraph (60 words). Format as a 5-row recipe matrix + intro essay (200 words). Constraints: avoid fake-meat and fake-cheese substitutes that aren't accessible in tier-2 India; suggest jaggery vs sugar substitution rationally; cite ICMR-NIN data for macros where possible.

prompts.title: Editorial Calendar for 90 Days (Q3 2026)
prompts.prompt: Act as a content strategist for a top-50 Indian food blog with daily readership 18,000. Build a 90-day editorial calendar for Jul-Sep 2026. Each week needs: (1) 1 deep recipe (1,200 words, full SEO build), (2) 1 quick recipe (500 words, mobile-first), (3) 1 essay/restaurant review (1,000 words), (4) 2 Insta posts + 3 reels + 1 carousel, (5) 1 newsletter edition, (6) 1 YouTube short. Themes by month: Jul = Monsoon Comforts, Aug = Independence Day Regional Foods, Sep = Festive Curtain-Raiser (Onam, Ganesh Chaturthi, Janmashtami). Provide: (a) weekly grid with specific dish ideas, (b) cross-promotion plan (blog -> Insta -> YT), (c) repurposing pipeline, (d) collab/sponsor slots (max 2/month), (e) KPI per channel (traffic, engagement, save rate). Format as a planning sheet. Constraints: no recipe repetition within 60 days; Hinglish title experiments allowed; tag every piece to monetisation pathway.

prompts.title: Hyperlocal SEO Article Strategy
prompts.prompt: Act as a SEO consultant with 10 years working on Indian food publishers (Burrp, NDTV Food, Cleartrip Food, Slurrp). Plan a hyperlocal SEO article series for 'Best Restaurants in Indiranagar Bengaluru'. Build: (1) 6-article cluster (pillar + sub-articles by cuisine type — South Indian / North Indian / Asian / Cafe / Fine-dining / Family-friendly), (2) keyword set per article (primary + secondary + long-tail), (3) on-page SEO outline (H1, H2 hierarchy, FAQ, internal links), (4) competitor SERP analysis approach (top 3 ranking sites for each keyword), (5) E-E-A-T signals (author bio, photo proof, visit dates, on-site Insta), (6) link-building approach (guest posts, local backlinks), (7) distribution (email + Insta + Bengaluru Reddit + Quora), (8) measurement (rank-tracking, CTR, dwell time). Format as a strategy doc + per-article brief template. Constraints: must visit each restaurant before writing; no paid placement disguised as organic; 200-word minimum per restaurant entry.

prompts.title: Anonymous Restaurant Review Code of Ethics
prompts.prompt: Act as a senior Indian restaurant critic with 14 years at major dailies (HT Brunch, ET Food). Draft a code of ethics for our newsroom's anonymous restaurant review programme. Cover: (1) anonymity practice (booking under different name, paying own bill, no special-table acceptance), (2) financial disclosure (newsroom reimbursement, no acceptance of free meals or comped drinks, no investments in F&B), (3) visit cadence (minimum 2 visits before publishing, 2 weeks apart), (4) conflict-of-interest matrix (relatives, friends, competing publications), (5) factual standards (verify ownership, chef credit, ingredient claims), (6) errata + correction policy, (7) review of negative reviews by editor before publishing, (8) ASCI advisory and consumer-protection law compliance. Format as a 2-page newsroom policy. Constraints: align with NCAER / Press Council of India norms; signed by every reviewer; reviewable annually; named legal counsel for libel.

prompts.title: Indian Festival Recipe Roundup Article
prompts.prompt: Act as a food editor at an Indian English digital publication (think VICE India / The Better India). Write a 1,200-word festival roundup article: '12 Lesser-Known Diwali Sweets Across India'. Structure: (1) opener (90 words: positioning the article as beyond ladoo and barfi), (2) per-region sweet (12 sections: Goa - Doce de Grão, Kerala - Achappam, Karnataka - Obbattu, Maharashtra - Anarsa, Bengal - Narikol Naru, Gujarat - Mohanthal, Punjab - Pinni, UP - Imarti, MP - Lavang Latika, Rajasthan - Ghevar, Sindh - Singdano Halwo, Tamil Nadu - Mysore Pak — adapt as needed). Each section: 70 words, includes one home-cook quote, ingredient hero, where to find it. (3) closing essay (90 words on identity + memory). Provide: (a) headline + subheadline, (b) hero image alt-text, (c) suggested infographic. Format as a publish-ready article. Constraints: respect cultural naming; verify regional ownership of dishes; no recipe instructions (this is editorial, not how-to).

prompts.title: Podcast Episode Outline for Food Show
prompts.prompt: Act as a podcast producer with 6 years at Indian food podcasts (think 'The Real Food Podcast' / 'Eat the World'). Build a 35-minute episode outline for 'The Spice Trail' podcast — episode topic: 'Why Hyderabadi Biryani Smells Different'. Cover: (1) cold open (60 sec — sensory teaser + the episode question), (2) host intro + setup (90 sec), (3) interview with food historian Pushpesh Pant (8 min — research, not a freewheel), (4) segment with chef from Paradise / Bawarchi (10 min — saffron, basmati, dum-cooking science), (5) sound walk through Char Minar food street (5 min — ambient + local interviews), (6) listener Q&A (4 min — 3 questions), (7) closing reflection + CTA (3 min). Provide: (a) detailed minute-by-minute beat sheet, (b) interview questions for each guest (10 each), (c) sound design notes, (d) episode title + description (Apple Podcasts), (e) social media promo plan. Format as a producer's episode bible. Constraints: research-led, not anecdote-led; obtain release forms; minimum 3 sources per claim.

prompts.title: Negative Review Diplomatic Phrasing Guide
prompts.prompt: Act as a senior food writer with 11 years across Indian publications. I had a poor experience at a Mumbai 5-star buffet (₹2,400/pax). I want to write an honest negative review without being unprofessional or risking defamation. Build me a phrasing guide that converts harsh observations into diplomatic-but-honest critique. Examples: 'food was cold' → 'plating temperature did not match the buffet's ambition'; 'rude waiter' → 'service warmth fluctuated'; 'overpriced' → 'price-to-experience ratio strained the value contract'. Provide 25 such conversions across food, service, ambience, value, and recovery. Plus: (a) 5 phrases to NEVER use (e.g., 'inedible'), (b) 5 honest-but-firm openers, (c) 5 closing-line frameworks that leave room for improvement, (d) defamation pitfalls in Indian context (Section 499/500 IPC), (e) editor sign-off checklist. Format as a writers' style sheet. Constraints: stay factual, never libellous; offer the restaurant a right-to-reply window before publishing.

prompts.title: Email Pitch to Food Editors (Freelance Writer)
prompts.prompt: Act as a freelance Indian food writer with 3 published features. Write a personalised email pitch to the food editor of Mint Lounge / Vogue India / Conde Nast Traveller proposing a 1,500-word feature: 'The Quiet Renaissance of Indian Vegetable Cookery in Mid-Scale Restaurants'. Email should: (1) open with a 30-word hook tied to a recent piece they've published (research them first), (2) one-paragraph pitch with 3 specific restaurants + 1 chef interview already lined up (Coriander Leaf, Yauatcha, Olive Mumbai), (3) why this story matters now (changing dietary culture, plant-forward economics), (4) my qualification (1 line + 2 clip links), (5) timeline + word count + fee expectation (₹15-20K), (6) sign-off + portfolio link + contact. Provide a 3-line follow-up email if no response in 10 days. Format as a ready-to-send email. Constraints: respect the editor's time; avoid generic 'I love your magazine'; unique angle backed by reportable sources; tone confident not pleading.

prompts.title: Ingredient Sourcing Guide for Readers
prompts.prompt: Act as a recipe blogger with 200K monthly readers. Write a sourcing guide article for 'Where to Buy Authentic Indian Cooking Ingredients in Indian Tier-2 Cities' (Indore, Lucknow, Coimbatore, Bhubaneswar, Vizag). Structure: (1) intro essay (120 words: tier-2 reader frustration), (2) per-city section (5 sections × 200 words each: best mandi for spices, best dairy, best regional pickle, best grain wholesaler, online alternative if local missing), (3) brand recommendation table (15 trusted Indian brands across spices, lentils, ghee, oil, jaggery — with rationale), (4) freshness check tips (5 tips for cardamom, saffron, asafoetida, chilli powder), (5) reader Q&A (3 sourcing questions), (6) closing (CTA to email regional sourcing tips). Format as a publish-ready article. Constraints: name actual mandis (verify spelling, location); avoid promotion of one brand over another without rationale; include online options like BigBasket, JioMart, MTR.

prompts.title: Brand Collaboration Brief Document
prompts.prompt: Act as a food creator manager with 6 years at Indian creator agencies (OML, Monk-E). Draft a brand collaboration brief between me (food creator, 350K Insta + 150K YT) and a tea brand (Vahdam Tea / Tetley / Tata Tea Premium). Deliverables: 1 reel (60 sec), 1 YouTube long (12 min), 4 stories. Brief should specify: (1) campaign objective and brand metric KPI, (2) key brand messages (3 must-says), (3) creative liberty zone (what I can experiment with), (4) deliverable spec (resolution, audio, hashtag, CTA, bio link), (5) timeline (briefing, shoot, edit, approval, post), (6) usage rights (paid media boost duration), (7) ASCI #ad disclosure language, (8) payment terms (50% advance, 50% on go-live, INR + 18% GST), (9) revision rounds (max 2), (10) exclusivity (no competing tea brand for 60 days). Format as a signed-quote document. Constraints: protect creative integrity; honest about audience demographic; one DPDP-compliant data sharing clause.

prompts.title: Behind-the-Scenes Vlog Content Plan
prompts.prompt: Act as a food vlogger with 600K YT subscribers (think Kabita's Kitchen / Nisha Madhulika BTS style). Plan a 6-episode behind-the-scenes vlog series — 'The Recipe Diaries' — taking viewers into kitchen development of a regional cuisine cookbook I'm writing. Each episode 8-12 min: (1) ep 1 - recipe testing day with mistakes, (2) ep 2 - sourcing trip to a mandi, (3) ep 3 - photoshoot day with photographer, (4) ep 4 - taste-test panel feedback, (5) ep 5 - publisher meeting, (6) ep 6 - launch day. For each episode: (a) cold open hook, (b) 3 act structure, (c) emotional beat (a stumble, a discovery, a moment), (d) thumbnail concept + title, (e) shoot crew + cost (₹), (f) cross-promotion (Insta reel cut + Twitter thread), (g) success KPI (CTR + watch time + comment vibe). Format as a series bible. Constraints: tone authentic-vulnerable not curated; budget ₹4 lakh total series; release every 2 weeks; no sponsor for first 3 episodes (build audience trust).

prompts.title: Insta Story Sequence for Restaurant Visit
prompts.prompt: Act as an Instagram food creator with 130K followers. Build a 12-story sequence for a live restaurant visit at 'Toast & Tonic' (Bengaluru) tonight. Stories: (1) cover slide (location pin + a 'we're here' tease), (2) ambience pan (3 sec), (3) menu close-up (highlight a hidden gem), (4) order reveal Q-poll, (5) starter arriving — sensory caption, (6) main shot with sound, (7) chef interaction (15-sec selfie story), (8) cocktail moment with mixology trivia, (9) dessert with a 'must try' ranking, (10) bill & verdict (transparent INR), (11) ASCI disclosure if PR-comped (or 'paid by self'), (12) Save + DM CTA. Provide: (a) caption per story (under 30 words), (b) sticker types (poll, slider, quiz, location, music), (c) lighting + framing tip, (d) avoiding repetition with last 3 visits. Format as a story-by-story script. Constraints: vertical 9:16; archive stories to Highlight 'Bengaluru Eats'; avoid loud trending audio that overwhelms food sounds.

prompts.title: Regional Cuisine Deep-Dive Series Outline
prompts.prompt: Act as a culinary documentarian with 8 years researching Indian regional cuisines (think Kunal Vijayakar / Marryam Reshii). Outline a 6-part long-form series — 'Coastal Plate: Stories from India's Edge' — covering Konkan, Malabar, Andhra Coast, Sundarbans, Kachchh, Goa coastline. Each part 2,500 words + 18 photos. Per part: (1) 90-word opener (sensory + history), (2) geography of food (terrain, fish, spice), (3) anchor recipe with a home cook's name + village + age, (4) 3 supporting dishes (with chef quotes), (5) cultural ritual (festival or daily ritual tied to food), (6) one ingredient deep-dive (e.g., Bombay Duck, Toddy Vinegar), (7) where it's headed (climate change, fisheries decline, urbanisation), (8) reader try-this section. Format as a series bible + per-essay brief. Constraints: ground-reporting required (visit each region); 3 sources per claim; respect community sensitivity (Sundarbans = Bonbibi tradition); pitch as long-form to Mint, FiftyTwo, Lounge.

prompts.title: Food Tour Itinerary Blog Post
prompts.prompt: Act as a food travel writer with 5 years at Outlook Traveller / Conde Nast India. Write a 1,400-word 'One Day Food Tour of Old Delhi' itinerary blog post for first-time visitors. Structure: (1) intro hook (110 words: chaotic charm + your relationship with the area), (2) 8 stops timed across the day (breakfast, mid-morning, lunch, post-lunch, evening, dinner, dessert, late-night chai) with names, addresses, must-try item, INR price, time-to-spend, photo-op tip, (3) walking distance + alternate metro route, (4) practical block (shoes, weather, crowd, queue strategy), (5) 3 cultural etiquettes (e.g., asking before photographing), (6) 4 hidden gems most blogs miss, (7) closing reflection (90 words). Format as a publish-ready blog post with 8 H2 stop headers + summary table. Constraints: walk-tested route; price band ₹50-₹500/stop; respect women-solo-traveller safety with specific advice; include a Hindi-English greeting glossary.

prompts.title: Affiliate Disclosure + Monetisation Audit
prompts.prompt: Act as a creator-monetisation auditor with 7 years across Indian food creators (Cookd, Hebbar's Kitchen, Sanjyot Keer). Audit my food blog + Insta + YT for affiliate disclosure compliance and monetisation health. Cover: (1) ASCI Influencer Code 2021 compliance (#ad, #sponsored, #partner placement and visibility), (2) FTC compliance (for global brand collaborations), (3) DPDP 2023 user-data handling on website forms, (4) GST registration health (turnover threshold breached?), (5) contract templates for brand collabs (named clauses to include — exclusivity, usage rights, indemnity, termination), (6) revenue mix audit (ad / affiliate / brand / digital product / consultation) with health benchmarks, (7) tax filing readiness (income heads, expense documentation), (8) recommended cleanup list (top 5 items). Format as a compliance + monetisation report. Constraints: assume turnover ₹38 lakh; recommend chartered accountant tier; flag if ITR-3 or ITR-4 applies; data factual not vibes-based.

prompts.title: Reader Comment Engagement Playbook
prompts.prompt: Act as a community manager for an Indian food publisher (think Slurrp / NDTV Food / Cooks Unplugged). Build a reader comment engagement playbook for our blog (15K monthly comments) and Instagram (avg 2,500 comments/post). Cover: (1) tone-of-voice guide (warm but expert; no defensive replies; humour OK), (2) response priority matrix (high-engagement / corrections / complaints / spam), (3) template responses for 6 common comment types (recipe substitution question, nutrition question, regional dispute over authenticity, translation request, cooking failure complaint, off-topic), (4) escalation rules (when to involve editor, legal, content moderator), (5) DPDP-aligned data privacy when comments include personal info, (6) banned words / profanity moderation list, (7) repeat-commenter loyalty layer (badge + Insta DM thank-you), (8) measurement dashboard (response rate, NPS, conversion to email subscriber). Format as an internal handbook. Constraints: Hindi/English bilingual responses where needed; never delete genuine criticism; published response time SLA: 4 hours within IST 9-9.
